Text: Decision adopted by the Committee under the Optional Protocol to the International Covenant on Economic, Social and Cultural Rights, concerning communication No. 236/2021[[fn:1]]*, [[fn:2]]**

Footnotes.text: *Adopted by the Committee at its seventy-seventh session (10–28 February 2025).

**The following members of the Committee participated in the examination of the communication: Aslan Abashidze, Lazhari Bouzid, Asraf Ally Caunhye, Laura-Maria Crăciunean-Tatu, Charafat El Yedri Afailal, Peters Sunday Omologbe Emuze, Santiago Manuel Fiorio Vaesken, Ludovic Hennebel, Joo-Young Lee, Karla Vanessa Lemus de Vásquez, Seree Nonthasoot, Giuseppe Palmisano, Laura Elisa Pérez, Julieta Rossi, Preeti Saran and Michael Windfuhr.

Text: 1. The authors of the communication allege that the State party has violated their rights under article 11 (1) of the Covenant by evicting them without providing them with alternative housing.

2. The Committee, acting through its working group on communications, registered the communication and requested the State party to take interim measures to suspend the authors’ eviction pending consideration of the communication or to provide them with adequate alternative housing, in genuine and effective consultation with them.
3. The State party submitted its observations on the communication, which were transmitted to the authors for comments.

4. At its meeting on 14 February 2025, the Committee, having taken note of the State party’s request for discontinuance on the grounds that the order for the authors’ eviction became statute-barred in June 2022 and is no longer enforceable and that, should a new eviction order be issued, the authors could submit a new communication to the Committee with a request for interim measures, decided to discontinue the consideration of communication No. 236/2021, in accordance with article 18 of its rules of procedure under the Optional Protocol.
